title: ED pediatric injury documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.
assessment.rationale: The exact WHO/ICRC trauma history supports onset, mechanism, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, risk context and caregiver concerns for pediatric injury without diagnosing the injury.

source.title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured
source.population: Adults and children with acute illness or injury presenting to first-contact emergency care.
source.setting: First-contact emergency units and resource-limited emergency-care settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for structured trauma history, secondary survey, extremity perfusion, and open-wound assessment; partial for minor outpatient injury follow-up, imaging, closure, antibiotics, and UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.

assessment.text: Supports inspection for wounds, bruising, swelling, tenderness, deformity, range of movement, neurological findings, severe pain, and other injury signs.
assessment.rationale: The exact secondary survey supports measured vital data, manual focused examination, injury severity, appearance, time-critical findings and existing tests only when assessed or reviewed.

source.title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured
source.population: Adults and children with acute illness or injury presenting to first-contact emergency care.
source.setting: First-contact emergency units and resource-limited emergency-care settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for structured trauma history, secondary survey, extremity perfusion, and open-wound assessment; partial for minor outpatient injury follow-up, imaging, closure, antibiotics, and UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports inspection for wounds, bruising, swelling, tenderness, deformity, range of movement, neurological findings, severe pain, and other injury signs.

assessment.text: Supports child-centred, private and accessible communication, consent to examination, and recording actual words and disagreements.
assessment.rationale: The exact NICE section supports consent to examination, child-centred communication and completed risk or consent discussion only when performed.

source.title: Child abuse and neglect
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; UAE safeguarding law and pathways govern local use
source.population: Children and young people under 18, including unborn babies, who may be at risk of or experiencing abuse or neglect, and their parents or carers.
source.setting: Health, emergency, social-care and other services encountering children and families.
source.exclusions: Exact for child-centred communication, consent to examination, recording actual words, non-leading exploration, observing the child and relationships, and clinician-led safeguarding action. It does not assert abuse or replace UAE reporting requirements.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports child-centred, private and accessible communication, consent to examination, and recording actual words and disagreements.

assessment.text: Supports observing the child and interactions, communicating directly and privately where appropriate, non-leading exploration and structured family or care-environment context.
assessment.rationale: The exact child-assessment section supports documenting safeguarding concern, clinician plan, specialist discussion and arranged handover only when concern was assessed and action completed; it does not assert abuse.
